spec_match.rationale: The page successfully demonstrates SpeechRecognition transcription and shows how the standard Web Speech API is used.

interactive.rationale: Features a live microphone dictation control, a status card grid, and real-time output text streams.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the index blurb perfectly, introducing feature detection and configuration of on-device vs cloud.

edge_cases.rationale: The page uses a naive platform platform check (navigator.userAgent) to declare on-device support, leading to a false positive in older Chrome versions on desktop where the ondevice property doesn't exist.

references.rationale: Explicitly references the ChromeStatus entry link in the 'see also' block.

design_system.rationale: Follows showcase CSS rules, utilizing clean spacing and standard variables for background paper and status values.

openQuestions.title: Refine on-device feature detection to check API capability
openQuestions.detail: Instead of blindly checking navigator.userAgent to declare 'on-device support' (which triggers false positives in older desktop Chrome versions), check if the 'ondevice' property is present on a dummy SpeechRecognition instance or its prototype (e.g. 'ondevice' in SpeechRecognition.prototype). If not present, clearly notify the developer that local on-device speech is unavailable in their current browser version.
